late_blight.name: Late Blight
late_blight.application: Spray every 7-10 days during wet season
late_blight.description: Most devastating potato disease. Acts fast in cold, humid conditions.

early_blight.name: Early Blight
early_blight.application: Apply at first sign of disease
early_blight.description: Common in mature plants. Shows characteristic concentric rings on leaves.

bacterial_wilt.name: Bacterial Wilt
bacterial_wilt.application: Prevention only - use certified disease-free seeds, practice crop rotation, maintain field sanitation
bacterial_wilt.safety: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately to prevent spread
bacterial_wilt.description: Soil-borne disease with no cure. Focus on prevention.

soft_rot.name: Soft Rot
soft_rot.application: Reduce moisture, improve ventilation, sort and remove infected tubers
soft_rot.safety: Handle tubers carefully to avoid wounds that allow bacterial entry
soft_rot.description: Post-harvest bacterial disease. Proper storage is key.

brown_rot.name: Brown Rot
brown_rot.application: Soil treatment before planting, use disease-free seeds
brown_rot.safety: Cannot cure infected plants. Focus on prevention and sanitation.
brown_rot.description: Bacterial disease similar to bacterial wilt. Prevention is critical.

healthy_leaf.name: Healthy Leaf
healthy_leaf.application: Continue regular monitoring and good agricultural practices
healthy_leaf.safety: Maintain crop health through proper nutrition and water management
healthy_leaf.description: Your potato leaves are healthy! Keep up the good work.

healthy_tuber.name: Healthy Tuber
healthy_tuber.application: Maintain proper storage conditions: cool (7-10°C), dark, well-ventilated
healthy_tuber.description: Your potato tubers are healthy and ready for storage or market.
